description: Add a --tui flag to inspect_layout.js that launches an interactive terminal UI showing a spatial wireframe of the layout — a visual map of the page rendered in the terminal, not a JSON tree. The layout JSON already contains pixel coordinates (rect.x, rect.y, rect.width, rect.height) and colors for every node. Scale these coordinates to fit the terminal window size (use process.stdout.columns / process.stdout.rows), then draw each layout region as a labeled box at its proportional position using ANSI escape codes or a terminal canvas library. Regions that overlap should render child regions on top of parent regions (paint in tree order: root first, children after). Each box should show its label text (node.label) and ideally its background color as an ANSI 256-color or truecolor background fill. Use the 'blessed' npm library installed into the skill's own node_modules at /workspaces/skills/layout-inspecting/scripts/node_modules/ via npm install --prefix /workspaces/skills/layout-inspecting/scripts blessed. The existing self-install bootstrap (lines 38-83 of inspect_layout.js) should be extended to auto-install blessed. Press 'q' to quit. The TUI renders the already-computed layout JSON in memory — no second browser fetch. --tui is incompatible with --output (print an error if both are given).
note: Added --tui flag to inspect_layout.js: extended ensureDependencies to auto-install blessed, added tui option parsing, incompatibility check with --output, and renderTui() function that flattens the layout tree in DFS pre-order, scales pixel coords to terminal dimensions, draws each region as a labeled blessed box with ANSI hex background color, with 'q' to quit
acceptance_criteria: Running the script with --tui launches an interactive terminal UI instead of printing JSON
The TUI renders a spatial wireframe: each layout region appears at its proportional position in the terminal, sized proportionally to the terminal dimensions
Regions are drawn in tree order so child regions visually appear on top of parent regions
Each region displays its label text and uses an ANSI background color approximating the node's background
The blessed library is auto-installed to the skill's own node_modules on first use, following the existing bootstrap pattern
--tui is incompatible with --output (print an error if both are given)

verification.value: Run: node /workspaces/skills/layout-inspecting/scripts/inspect_layout.js https://example.com --tui. Verify the terminal shows a spatial map of the page — boxes at proportional positions with labels and colored backgrounds, not a JSON tree or text outline. Press q to exit.
